summarization.a: The subfigure shows the surface nitrogen network highlighting available N atoms for nucleophilic attack by −SiH2Cl. Different nitrogen environments (NR, N1°, and N2°) are marked to distinguish their local coordination and reactivity.
summarization.b: The inset shows a close-up atomic configuration of the >SiH2 species after bond formation, emphasizing the local N–Si–N bonding geometry.

a.question: How does subfigure (a) identify nitrogen atoms that are susceptible to nucleophilic attack by −SiH2Cl?
a.answer: Subfigure (a) labels different nitrogen environments on the surface, distinguishing reactive NR sites from N1° and N2° configurations. This labeling highlights which nitrogen atoms are geometrically and chemically accessible for nucleophilic attack by −SiH2Cl.

a.question: Which nitrogen site is directly involved in bonding with −SiH2Cl during the nucleophilic attack?
Does this figure help distinguish reactive nitrogen sites from less reactive ones on the surface?

b.question: What bonding angle is explicitly highlighted in the inset of subfigure (b)?
